/.title: Improving the California digital experience
/.content:
california’s office digital innovation dedicated improving experiences all californians

’re guided by following principles:

  • start with user needs
  • less
  • design data research intuition experimentation
  • hard work make simple elegant
  • iterate then iterate again
  • make accessible inclusive
  • understand context
  • build services websites
  • consistent uniform
  • make things open: makes better
  • prioritize security respect privacy
  • optimize performance
put these principles into action three areas:

  • < href="https://cagovgithubio/covid19cagovsitehandbook//design/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">design
  • < href="https://cagovgithubio/covid19cagovsitehandbook/content/" noreferrer">content
  • < href="https://cagovgithubio/covid19cagovsitehandbook/tech/" noreferrer">tech

/content/principles.html.title: Content principles
/content/principles.html.content:
our core principle write content that’s usercentered ’s:

  • clear
  • concise
  • accessible
achieve this through variety methods

voice

source truthexpressing opinions
information providersservice providers
welcoming everyoneoverbearing
friendlyfake
officialcold distant
conversationaldisrespectful
straightforwardblunt
empatheticapathetic
sensitive toward otherspretending save people know everything about them

focus needs services

give what they need direct them services your website telling think government agency

requirements advice

people come find out give advice make suggestions use should little possible

plain language

plain language clear accessible everyone including readers with disabilities limited english fluency

  • use < href="https://plainlanguagegov/guidelines/words/usesimplewordsphrases/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">simple words when possible
    • sacrifice clarity simplicity
  • explain jargon have
  • spell acronyms first used page experience later put acronym parentheses after use
    • better known than full name instead assume because ’s familiar workplace that everyone knows default spelling acronym
  • use positive phrases instead negative ones they’re easier understand
    • example: lack have

clear sentences

  • use href="https://plainlanguagegov/guidelines/conversational/useactivevoice/" noreferrer">active voice strong verbs
    • href="https://plainlanguagegov/guidelines/words/avoidhiddenverbs/" noreferrer">hidden verbs
  • turn gerunds into verbs where makes sense
    • example: help helping
  • write present tense
  • pronouns like this those these references above vague restate ’re referring
  • write abbreviate months dates some cultures interpret 9/5/2020 may 9 2020 september 5 2020
  • use numerals numbers
    • only go one decimal place only decimals
    • use commas numbers over 999 helps understand order magnitude

reading level

write sixth grade level lower able reach goal look opportunities simpler words shorter sentences check reading levels href="http://hemingwayappcom/" noreferrer">hemingway editor eliminate very hard read sentences minimize sentences

concise

use short paragraphs more all regardless level

  • have thought per sentence
  • vary lengths paragraphs sound natural
  • remove add value
    • example: say please reader
  • confident

organize by importance

put most important page often applies people least (like specific groups) goes last

keep conversational

write how speak writing loud hear sounds

  • imagine each period breath comma pause
  • use common contractions ’ll ’s ’ll
    • “” don’t aren’t skimming see same
    • uncommon this’ll y’all ain’t
  • where sense start show relationship between smooth transition
  • refer agency don’t unclear refers writer
recommend ap style exception: serial (also called oxford comma) reduce confusion comes before : brought apples bananas oranges

accessibility

all californians

keep following mind write:

  • californians financial resources
  • californians without internet access
  • californians who live difficult housing situations those domestic violence)
  • californians struggling depression suicide
  • californians fixed address
  • californians disabilities
  • communities color
  • immigrant californians
  • tribal communities
  • californians fluency
  • rural californians
when discussing benefits supports state:

  • minimum requirements
  • any income restrictions
  • available immigration status including:
    • how their personal information protected
    • which immigrants are eligible
    • counts under public charge rule
audience consider languages should translated into

get site

build google analytics search console trying site using modify metadata mirror language

work user researchers deeper testing well can complete tasks also discover about

/tech/fast-dashboards/.title: Fully accessible dashboards
/tech/fast-dashboards/.content:
set goals our dashboards areas :

  • accessibility
  • performance
  • reliability
  • maintenance
< href="https://covid19cagov/equity/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">california’s commitment health equity example dashboard that meets goals evaluated several charting tools find one helped us meet used d3js all data visualizations this dashboard

why

biggest wins using d3 were:

  • raw performance: total weight code behind equity < 1% size tableau < href="https://covid19cagov/statedashboard/" noreferrer">state dashboard rendering time very fast compared tableau here’s side by comparison:
  • exceeded accessibility standards: charts elements can tabbed through they effectively with screenreader built like often make visualization itself accessible instead legal requirements short text summary outside visualization
  • complete translations: allowed translate whole chart down tooltips labels deep translation integration too difficult other tools
these are huge upgrades end user performance accessibility failures may unusable some californians

other benefits architecture currently state dev teams include:

  • data updates happen completely independently visual representation code
  • chart part existing front codebase means:
    • requires additional external services subscriptions introduces new possible points failure
    • inherits environment integrations lets pull from sets reviewed staging fullyvetted datasets production build system variables
    • chart javascript css any web developer troubleshoot modify without licenses
    • checked into same git repository rest site changes tracked standard rollback procedures available
    • any desired layout publishing workflow means automated test run against page updates
  • embed each its own component encapsulates execution class tested modified isolation quickly removed dropping import statement custom element

how

use library output svg charts encapsulate chart’s unique component

web components

components wrap separate required development browser’s method creating user interface elements allow define your html tags known associate class element browser will execute events associated discovered markup

here’s element:

source passed attribute url defined specific logic inside component receives componentspecific lifecycle access dom functions

google offers more info href="https://developersgooglecom/web/updates/2017/01/webcomponentsorghl=en" noreferrer">web components

d3

there many similar open libraries shipped sites highcharts there tons lightweight chartjs frappe

chose because :

  • source
  • requires license use
  • large feature small footprint
  • good documentation
  • strong community over years shares lot examples
most js type have basic measured kb these render much faster users than while allows drag drop create visualizations outputting sizes mb

translations

integrated pipeline humanpowered service href="http://covid19cagov" noreferrer">covid19cagov that’s made integrate strings labels legends site services

this let deliver presented user’s chosen language had never been able get layer way before d3driven href="http://covid19cagov/equity/" noreferrer">covid19cagov/equity/ provide better experience translated versions

above screenshot shows internal labels vietnamese

accessibility

d3 main document embedding them frames makes easier enable navigable mouse

techniques tabindex focus tab interact even can’t itself

/tech/performance/.title: Performance and accessibility
/tech/performance/.content:
people use variety devices range conditions when they try access web services this makes hard ensure service works well everybody review all big new features make sure everyone can them also have automated production monitoring catch regressions that sneak with minor bug fixes

new feature development

lighthouse audit tools available part chrome developer help initial performance accessibility audits shoot score 100 desktop 90 bandwidth+cpu throttled mobile integrated hints helps fix identified problems

also:

  • use screen readers ourselves
  • navigate through flow without using mouse
  • emulate vision deficiencies (available dev tools rendering panel)
  • get user testing results from people who assistive tech regularly can

production monitoring

find problems lighthouse siteimprove calibre

calibre our most useful tool runs automatically every few hours logs audits reports any changes slack caught introduced accidentally calibre

/tech/serverless/.title: Serverless APIs
/tech/serverless/.content:
< href="http://covid19cagov" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">covid19cagov site runs dozens builds day release normal data content updates widgets that depend more dynamic datasets make calls backend apis retrieve query results

use functions service (faas) run code serverless pattern infrastructure azure functions this similar aws lambda faas provides runtime which ephemeral

these tools are fastest way scale down response varying load new instances created quickly requests destroyed when idle few minutes all scaling management part cloud platform frees developers from managing orchestration any upkeep environment

benefits

  • built scaling
  • built management
  • fastest let ramp based request volume
  • low cost
  • faster development workflows

use cases

serverless patterns appropriate longrunning computationally intensive tasks these programs efficiently longerlived platforms with flexible cpu/memory configurations shines bursty high volume short workloads like receiving data querying source transforming returning json

outsourcing complexity

workflow speed wins due developer convenience able build api by writing function contains mostly business logic deploy seconds solid allows them focus team goals instead complexity

/tech/ssg/.title: Static site pregeneration
/tech/ssg/.content:
combine authoring wordpress with deployment using 11ty static site generating build step this gives us mature refined content management tool bulletproof low hassle production architecture

biggest benefit pregeneration reduces infrastructure required host web production allows server replaced by s3 like file backed cdn

other benefits include:

  • less single points failure production
  • reduced attack surface
  • cheaper cloud hosting cost
  • built cross region scalability
  • higher sla from provider
  • portability ( can switch cms tools providers)
set workflows that listen changes our pull updates into github repository each change repository triggers git action workflow which runs tests before merges merged trigger deploy task run regenerates entire deploys target environment

technology that supports state websites critical delivering accessible services all californians deliver technologically great through:

  • delivering fast web sites using href="/tech/ssg/" noreferrer">static site generation allow us render pages advanced functionality with small code footprints experience retains quality even users less expensive devices
  • prioritizing speed accessibility when building highly dynamic dashboards href="https://covid19cagov/equity/" noreferrer">equity dashboard example making sure translations screen reader navigation time interaction can achieved while href="/tech/fastdashboards/" noreferrer">complex data visualizations
  • architecting systems require smaller production footprints use href="/tech/serverless/" noreferrer">serverless api patterns run resilient scalable hardware these practices let handle bursty traffic low cost get higher slas from cloud providers operations management staff
  • integrating href="/tech/performance/" noreferrer">continuous testing make maintain functional accessibility performance goals each change

/content/types/page-template.html.title: Page style template
/content/types/page-template.html.content:
discovered through user testing that common page template creates consistency help people find information they’re looking

toplevel message

start with brief sentence states most important takeaway page this sets their expectations about what they’ll ’s valuable them there could second absolutel needed that’s limit making bigger emphasizes its importance

here’s example from < href="https://covid19cagov/travel/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">covid19cagov travel page:

travel increases your risk getting covid19 infecting others reason california department public health asks limit travel during pandemic

page index

way users easily long add index top allows see what’s glance select section they need

  • start text introduces index like “ will find:”
  • make bulleted bolded list h2 headers link subheaders something enough appear elevate header
  • make each header anchor link item corresponding header
  • keep 6 items less fit smaller ideas under larger umbrella ideas

action

great action

< href="https://cagovgithubio/covid19cagovsitehandbook/static/img/travelpageindexjpg" target="_self">

/content/formatting.html.title: Formatting content
/content/formatting.html.content:
wellformatted content easy read accessible

headings

clear headings make pages easier scan understand

  • use sentence case capitalize first word proper nouns ’s friendlier helps readers recognize nouns
  • make name your page h1 header only use one header per page
    • good tells people what about they’ll find example apply unemployment insurance
    • make concise prepare wildfire instead steps prepare wildfire
  • use levels ascending numerical order skip steps ok repeat levels
  • exception: questions accordion format are always h4 format matter size gone before
  • always have least line text between headers
  • tags full sentences nonheader body text
check heading structure google docs select view show document outline confirm they nest appropriately

lead class text

lead makes larger explain service topic top page underneath h1 paragraph (one two max) lead page this code create text:

titles

title appears when hover over tab browser users change tabs their browser

make followed by hyphen site name gives understanding here’s including coding tags:

page urls

use url search engines page

replace spaces with hyphens can them delete conjunctions prepositions articles long url still keeps same meaning

  • titled request birth certificate would become /requestbirthcertificate
  • into /preparewildfire different meaning
  • feels too long consider shortening page’s sure both give enough detail know about
use map build certificate lives under called services : < href="http://alphacagov/services/requestbirthcertificate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">alphacagov/services/requestbirthcertificate

any time spelled out content hyperlink homepage

bold italics underlining

make important information keywords bold italics emphasis

underline content web browsers highlight links automatically underlining nonlink confuses readers

links buttons

use button form something user wants embedded standing alone

  • link webpages pdfs much possible more accessible across devices easily searched less likely break
    • must link document pdf documents excel sheets powerpoint presentations require software view them that may have
    • indicate going file providing its extension (like pdf doc ppt) choose want download
  • make match destination arrived right place
  • when creating button short descriptive distinctive
  • start active verb like apply submit search focus user’s needs
  • see more here view more idea where go these phrases also assume everyone see
  • limit number readable several relevant links put bulleted list after main text
  • have support comprehension disrupt until alright send away (after ’ve conveyed point)
  • open < href="https://wwww3org/tr/wcag20techs/g200html" noreferrer"> window open new window there’s reason href="https://wwww3org/tr/wcag20techs/g201html" noreferrer">warning will open

notes disclaimers

when info (especially data tables graphs) needs explanation follow note provide clarity smaller signal reader secondary info wordpress html text:

/content/types.html.title: Content types
/content/types.html.content:
some content easier understand nonparagraph form bullet points alerts info boxes also make pages scan

bullet points

use points list items that are related only use there more than one item list

put period end point ’s full sentence

  • ask family friends contractors they recommend
  • search online read reviews consider source
  • repairs your homeowners insurance might cover cost suggest
add sentence capitalize first word every point
ask contractor share name their carrier copies both their:

  • workers’ compensation insurance
  • general liability insurance
consistent with whether sentences

always each point

numbered lists

use numbered lists when user needs tasks certain order like process they’re especially helpful steps happen across different agencies

  • start task verb
  • provide highlevel summary task put smaller details show/hide text
  • keep description short few paragraphs
    • cannot condensed guide instead
  • give number < href="https://wwwa11y101com/development/arialabel" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">aria label screen readers can them aloud step one two
  • link already exists agency website duplicate prepare users information need before select link
example href="https://wwwalphacagov/hirelicensedcontractorhomeimprovements/" noreferrer">hire licensed home improvements

accordions

one way simpler shorter most people some expandable section call accordion coming page see title text icon lets know content

accordions used see good rule thumb applies less 50% page’s expected visitors ok accordion

accordions just page shorter everyone should content belongs main body page asking open accordion get risks missing have take action (clicking mouse tapping phone) which extra effort

keep short paragraphs communicate better served being body

questions answers

question answer format great address edge cases type accordion

when creating section questions answers any answers about financial help questions cluster around topics subheaders organize them

guides

comprehensive complex information usually spans multiple pages side navigation move between pages

href="https://wwwalphacagov/applyunemploymentinsurance/" noreferrer">apply unemployment insurance

smart answers

smart gives that’s specific location situation enter into two form fields

  • clear limits answer
    • example: look cities california front instead through error message
  • give button descriptive term anticipates will receive
    • example: check water enter
  • make messages proactive assigning blame
    • example: enter city california entered outside california
examples href="https://wwwalphacagov/findminimumwageyourcity/" noreferrer">find minimum wage city href="https://wwwalphacagov/checklaneclosures/" noreferrer">check lane closures trip

guided guided takes provide tailored them

  • when separate pages next helpful final convey outcome qualify
  • give back previous question from buttons questions
href="https://wwwalphacagov/checkcangetdiscountedphoneservice/" discounted phone service

checklists radio buttons

use checklist want able options list option

forms

forms allow needs

  • create titles field placeholder disappears starts typing hard remember what field ensure accurate accessible
  • display valid typing confirm choose
  • add helper appears someone makes invalid entry
    • example: please ninedigit number 5555555555
  • include explain results get
    • example: search city

alerts

alerts highlight important timesensitive deadlines

info boxes

put box help find quickly while skimming urgent alert instead

page indexes

easily long index top

  • start brief sentence states takeaway page
  • follow introduces index “ this find:”
  • make bulleted bolded h2 headers link subheaders something enough appear elevate header
  • make header anchor link its corresponding header
  • keep 6 less fit ideas under larger umbrella ideas

/design/principles.html.title: Design principles
/design/principles.html.content:
design principles help teams stakeholders align design vision decisionmaking

they give us shared understanding prioritize our next steps

principles are once again leveraging proven work best practices like 18f uk government digital services starting point ’re adopting iterating created by gds

< href="http://alphacagov" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">alphacagov include:

  • start with user needs
  • less
  • design data research intuition experimentation ( added “research intuition experimentation” few reasons data doesn’t always tell whole story also need yes own users californians inform design)
  • hard make simple elegant
  • iterate then iterate again
  • make accessible inclusive (< href="http://ukgov" noreferrer">ukgov went “this everyone” adapted more explicit reflect california)
  • understand context
  • build services websites
  • consistent uniform
  • make things open: makes better
  • prioritize security respect privacy (something new list recognition how incredibly important both security are)
  • optimize performance (another one slow load times cost people money that’s accessibility equity issue)
  • these will product design ’ll continue refine them learn doing

resources

< href="https://wwwgovuk/guidance/governmentdesignprinciples" noreferrer">govuk principles

< href="https://methods18fgov/decide/designprinciples/" noreferrer">18f principles
